description: Concurrent writes fail immediately with SQLITE_BUSY because busy_timeout is left at the default of zero.
body: Failure mode: two connections try to write; SQLite allows only one writer, and with busy_timeout=0 the second call returns SQLITE_BUSY instantly instead of waiting for the lock. Detection signals: intermittent SQLITE_BUSY under concurrency; no busy_timeout pragma in the connection setup; errors disappear when traffic is single-threaded. Do not paper over it with ad-hoc app-level retry loops alone. Safer fallback: set a non-zero busy_timeout on every connection, keep write transactions short, and use BEGIN IMMEDIATE for read-then-write to avoid the upgrade deadlock.
